concerns.text: FR-001 requires parsing 'explicit documentation mentions' for LLM adoption but provides no verifiable procedure or regex pattern to distinguish a mention from a general discussion. Without a deterministic rule (e.g., 'must appear in README.md or CONTRIBUTING.md within 500 chars of the phrase "LLM" or "Copilot"'), this requirement is not testable.

FR-002 defines 'iteration count' as 'update cycles' but does not specify the algorithm to derive this from PR metadata (e.g., 'count of push events between PR open and merge'). Without a precise definition, the metric cannot be reproducibly calculated or verified.

FR-003 mandates controlling for 'domain complexity' but does not define how this variable is measured or derived. Unlike 'lines of code' or 'contributor count', 'domain complexity' lacks a specified operationalization, making the regression model unverifiable.

SC-001 states dataset validity is measured against '≥ 10 pull requests in the last 12 months' but fails to specify the time window reference point (e.g., 'relative to the data ingestion date' or 'relative to the current date'). This ambiguity prevents deterministic validation of the dataset.

SC-002 requires regression models to include controls for 'domain complexity' but, like FR-003, fails to define the metric. A success criterion cannot be measured if the variable it depends on is undefined.
